channel.title: NYT > Business
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: India Is Moving Fast to Build A.I. Data Centers. A Coastal City May Pay the Price.
item.description: With India lagging in the technology, officials are embracing giant data centers. But critics say the megaprojects will use up energy and water, without providing long-term jobs.
item.dc:creator: Mujib Mashal and Hari Kumar
item.media:credit: Priyadarshini Ravichandran for The New York Times
item.media:description: The port city of Visakhapatnam in the state of Andhra Pradesh is rapidly transforming into India’s coastal gateway for global tech infrastructure, fueled by massive A.I. data centers.

item.title: Europe Wants to Break Free From American and Chinese Technology. But How?
item.description: France and Germany want to quit relying on America and China for key technology like artificial intelligence, but they’re having to choose where to do it.
item.dc:creator: Jim Tankersley and Mark Landler
item.media:description: Sébastien Lecornu, the French prime minister, has called for France to increase its independence from American technology firms, announcing plans to replace Zoom with a French-developed version.

item.title: Trump Administration to Impose New Tariffs on Brazil
item.description: The United States accused the country of unfair trade practices. The tariff will replace ones that the Supreme Court struck down.
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson and Ana Ionova
item.media:description: President Trump first imposed tariffs on Brazil last year in an effort to help former President Jair Bolsonaro avoid a jail sentence.

item.title: New York Times Files Motion to Quash Subpoenas of Its Journalists
item.description: The Justice Department is seeking to compel grand jury testimony as part of an investigation into Times reporting on the president’s new Air Force One jet.
item.dc:creator: Michael M. Grynbaum
item.media:description: President Trump boarding the new Air Force One at Joint Base Andrews in Maryland this month.

item.title: NextEra and Dominion File to Form a Huge Power Company
item.description: They asked regulators in three states and at a federal agency to approve a deal that would create a combined company with 10 million customers across the Southeast.
item.dc:creator: Ivan Penn
item.media:description: NextEra Energy’s headquarters in Juno Beach, Fla. Florida does not require regulatory approval of NextEra’s deal with Dominion Energy, but the companies filed requests with Virginia, North Carolina and South Carolina.

item.title: PayPal Receives $53 Billion Takeover Offer Involving Stripe
item.description: PayPal, which owns Venmo, is the target of what one group of analysts described as a “lowball” offer from Stripe, another payment processor, and Advent, a private equity firm.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Hirsch and Stacy Cowley
item.media:description: The share price of PayPal, which is led by Enrique Lores, has fallen 24 percent over the past year.

item.title: United Airlines Will Soon Let You Take Over the Middle Seat, for a Price
item.description: The option, set to go on sale on some flights later this year, will feature a shared table in the middle seat. The move may actually save the airline money.
item.dc:creator: Christine Chung
item.media:description: Passengers will able to book in rows with a shared table in the middle seat on some United Airlines flights starting later this year. Prices are not yet available.

item.title: Senators Grill Justice Dept. Officials on New York Times Subpoenas
item.description: Democrats questioned the acting attorney general, Todd Blanche, and a U.S. attorney about subpoenas issued to Times journalists who reported on security concerns with the president’s new plane.
item.dc:creator: Michael M. Grynbaum
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times, Alex Kent/The New York Times

item.title: Treasury to Begin Minting $1 Gold Trump Coin Featuring President’s Face
item.description: Critics question whether the coins are legal and argue that President Trump is using the currency to celebrate himself.
item.dc:creator: Alan Rappeport

item.title: SpaceX Briefly Falls Below I.P.O. Price of $135
item.description: After soaring in its stock market debut, the shares of Elon Musk’s rocket and A.I. company have been rocky.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mac and Lauren Hirsch

item.title: War Risk for Businesses Will Mean Higher Prices No Matter What Happens
item.description: Doing business in a more dangerous and unpredictable world will cost more, pushing up the price of everything from food to electronics.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Cohen
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Linh Pham for The New York Times
item.media:description: Supply chain disruptions have raised costs and disrupted manufacturing across Southeast Asia.

item.title: Goldman’s Former Top Lawyer Calls Epstein a ‘Masterful Liar’
item.description: Kathryn Ruemmler, the former general counsel of Goldman Sachs, testified on Wednesday in a closed-door congressional hearing investigating Jeffery Epstein.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Goldstein
item.category: House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform
item.media:credit: William B. Plowman/NBCUniversal, via Getty Images
item.media:description: Kathryn Ruemmler in 2014.

item.title: IBM’s Stock Crash Reignites Fears of A.I. Disruption in Software Sector
item.description: The company tumbled sharply after previewing quarterly results that were disappointing. It’s not good news for software and tech consulting companies.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Ross Sorkin, Bernhard Warner, Sarah Kessler, Michael J. de la Merced, Niko Gallogly, Brian O’Keefe and Ian Mount
item.media:description: A dire earnings preview from IBM’s chief executive, Arvind Krishna, spooked investors yesterday.

item.title: Oil Prices Tick Higher as U.S. Reinstates Iran Blockade
item.description: The United States reinstated its blockade on Iranian ports in the Strait of Hormuz and shipping slowed to a crawl amid the renewed warfare in the critical waterway.
item.dc:creator: Joe Rennison and Katie Robertson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: A Year After DOGE Cuts, Social Security Is Trying to Stabilize
item.description: The agency has been quietly hiring workers again, and its leadership is focused on improving technology to ease workloads left by the departure of about 7,800 staff members.
item.dc:creator: Tara Siegel Bernard
item.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Social Security Administration has reassigned many of its workers to staff its national 800 number, an effort to shorten wait times.

item.title: The American E.V. Has Been Crushed. Will It Take the U.S. Auto Industry With It?
item.description: The largest U.S. automakers have backed away from electric vehicles, even as global sales are booming. The decision may make them obsolete.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Shaer

item.title: Twitter Turns 20: Two Decades of Memes, Movements and Hot Takes
item.description: The first tweets from the public appeared on July 15, 2006, several months after a soft launch. It was rebranded as X after being acquired by Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: Neil Vigdor
item.media:credit: Thor Swift for The New York Times
item.media:description: On July 15, 2006, a new era of provocative hot takes, kneejerk reactions and random thoughts on social media ushered itself in with the public debut of Twitter, after a soft launch that March.

item.title: What Trump’s New Iran Blockade Could Mean for Oil Prices
item.description: The first blockade in April choked off Iranian exports without causing a lasting price surge. Now global oil reserves are lower, and ships face heightened risks.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Friedman and Rebecca F. Elliott
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The Strait of Hormuz carried about one-fifth of the world’s oil before the war, making it one of the most critical shipping lanes.

item.title: CXMT, China’s Chip Champion, to Raise Billions in Race for A.I. Control
item.description: CXMT, a linchpin in China’s drive to develop homegrown artificial intelligence technology, is aiming to raise nearly $10 billion in a blockbuster public offering in Shanghai.
item.dc:creator: Steve Lohr and Xinyun Wu
item.media:description: ChangXin Memory Technologies in Hefei. Little known outside of China, the 10-year-old company is benefiting from booming demand for memory chips.

item.title: China’s Economy Grows at Slowest Pace in Years
item.description: Economic growth of 4.3 percent in the second quarter, versus the same period last year, reflected a broad slump outside of the country’s export-oriented manufacturing might.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Stevenson and Murphy Zhao
item.media:credit: Qilai Shen for The New York Times
item.media:description: Shoppers at an electric car showroom in Shanghai. Chinese consumers have embraced a culture of thriftiness, as many are not feeling the benefits of economic growth.

item.title: Tech Investors and Longevity Influencers Back Neko Health’s Mega Round
item.description: The health tech start-up co-founded by Spotify’s Daniel Ek is worth billions more after a new $700 million funding round.
item.dc:creator: Bernhard Warner
item.media:credit: Sam Bush for The New York Times
item.media:description: Neko Health, founded by Hjalmar Nilsonne, left, and Daniel Ek, offers full-body diagnostic scans priced to attract more than just affluent patients.

item.title: Data Centers to Add Billions in Power Costs in 13 States
item.description: A power auction conducted by a giant grid operator is expected to add $6.3 billion in additional charges to consumers and businesses because of electricity needs of data centers.
item.dc:creator: Ivan Penn
item.media:description: Northern Virginia is home to several data centers, including the Digital Realty Data Center in Ashburn.

item.title: Public Health Groups Sue F.D.A. Over Flavored E-Cigarette Policy
item.description: Executives unhappy with the agency donated and lobbied President Trump directly. Major companies have already begun to prepare and ship new products.
item.dc:creator: Christina Jewett
item.media:description: Flavored electronic cigarette devices displayed for sale at a store in Pinecrest, Fla. in 2023.

item.title: Top Tax Official to Leave Trump Administration
item.description: Ken Kies, who held two top jobs at the Treasury Department and the I.R.S., is leaving the government.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Duehren

item.title: Trump Flip-Flops on Strait of Hormuz, Creating New Risks for Global Economy
item.description: The president now wants Gulf nations to invest in the U.S. in exchange for safe passage. Shipping industry experts worry other nations will follow that lead.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Gross
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross for The New York Times
item.media:description: A day after saying he would charge 20 percent fees to ships in exchange for U.S. military protection in the Strait of Hormuz, Mr. Trump said he wanted Gulf states to invest in the United States instead.

item.title: Chipotle Is Bringing Its Mexican-Inspired Fast Food to Monterrey, Mexico
item.description: The California-based restaurant chain is opening its first location south of the U.S.-Mexico border this week, bringing its bowls and burritos to Monterrey.
item.dc:creator: Claire Moses
item.media:credit: Amy Lombard for The New York Times
item.media:description: Even though there is no shortage of burritos and tacos in Mexico, Chipotle sees a market for its customizable burrito bowls and other menu offerings south of the U.S. border.

item.title: Leonard Abramson, Health Care Innovator and Philanthropist, Dies at 93
item.description: He created U.S. Healthcare, one of the first H.M.O.s, and used his great wealth from the sale of the company for causes like advancing cancer research.
item.dc:creator: Richard Sandomir
item.category: Abramson, Leonard and Madlyn, Familly Cancer Research Institute

item.title: Trump Paid $2 Million by South Korean Company Facing Trade Investigation
item.description: The payment illustrates the minefield Mr. Trump has created by maintaining personal financial ties with foreign businesses while he is in office.
item.dc:creator: Eric Lipton and John Yoon
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump maintains personal financial ties with nearly 30 different business ventures with counterparties worldwide.

item.title: Warren Buffet Declines to Donate to Gates Foundation Amid Epstein Revelations
item.description: The decision by the Berkshire Hathaway founder comes in the wake of Epstein revelations that have troubled the Gates Foundation.
item.dc:creator: Theodore Schleifer
item.media:description: Warren Buffett at Berkshire Hathaway annual shareholders’ meeting last year.

item.title: T. Rex Fossil Sells for $50.1 Million, Putting the King Back On Top
item.description: In a booming fossil market, the T. rex had been dethroned by a stegosaurus. No longer.
item.dc:creator: Julia Jacobs and Zachary Small
item.media:credit: Timothy A. Clary/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: The 12-and-a-half-foot-tall T. rex, known as Gus, on view at Sotheby’s ahead of its auction on Tuesday.

item.title: White House Cheers Inflation Data While Iran War Sparks New Price Surge
item.description: The news of an improvement in consumer prices in June came as the U.S. and Iran escalated attacks, risking a return to high energy prices.
item.dc:creator: Tony Romm
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Gas prices have reached above $3.85 per gallon nationally, according to AAA.

item.title: What to know about the Fed chairman’s testimony.
item.dc:creator: Colby Smith

item.title: The Fed’s new chairman is asking for advice.
item.description: Five new task forces are focused on topics that Kevin M. Warsh, the chairman of the Federal Reserve, has said are “central to the broad conduct of monetary policy.”
item.dc:creator: Colby Smith and Ben Casselman
item.media:description: Mervyn King, who led the Bank of England for a decade, helped usher in a more transparent era for the central bank.

item.title: Warsh Reiterates Fed’s Pledge to Get Inflation Down but Doesn’t Specify How
item.description: Kevin M. Warsh, in his first testimony to Congress as chairman of the Federal Reserve, did not shed light on whether he supported higher interest rates to tame price pressures.
item.dc:creator: Colby Smith
item.category: Federal Reserve Bank of New York
item.media:description: US Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh testifying during a House Financial Services Committee hearing on Capitol Hill today.

item.title: Paramount vs. the States
item.description: An effort to block the media giant’s planned takeover of Warner Bros. Discovery underscores how states are taking a more active role in antitrust regulation.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Ross Sorkin, Bernhard Warner, Sarah Kessler, Michael J. de la Merced, Niko Gallogly, Brian O’Keefe, Ian Mount and Lauren Hirsch
item.media:description: Paramount’s planned takeover of Warner Bros. Discovery has hit a new snag.

item.title: Chai Discovery, an A.I. Drug Start-Up, Raises $400 Million
item.description: The fund-raising values the company at $3.8 billion and underscores investor interest in using artificial intelligence to tackle problems like drug discovery.
item.dc:creator: Michael J. de la Merced
item.media:description: The A.I. drug discovery start-up Chai Discovery was founded by, from left, Matthew McPartlon, Joshua Meier, Jacques Boitreaud and Jack Dent.

item.title: Big Banks Smash Earnings Records, but ‘Tectonic’ Risks Loom
item.description: The quarterly results reflect the dichotomy between geopolitical uncertainty and the stable American economy.
item.dc:creator: Rob Copeland and Stacy Cowley
item.media:credit: Karsten Moran for The New York Times
item.media:description: The headquarters of JPMorgan Chase in Manhattan. The bank and its largest rivals reported a collective $49 billion in second-quarter profits,

item.title: Inflation Slowed During Pause in War With Iran
item.description: The Consumer Price Index fell to a 3.5 percent annual increase in June, and prices marked their biggest drop from a month earlier since 2020.
item.dc:creator: Ben Casselman and Colby Smith
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Workers unloading produce at a grocery store in Brooklyn, N.Y. in April. “The more oil stays elevated, the more you get second-round effects on things like airlines and transportation and even food prices,” said Michael Gapen, chief U.S. economist at Morgan Stanley.

item.title: China Purges Top Official Over Corruption and Sex Charges
item.description: Ma Xingrui is the third member of the Politburo to be purged since 2022. He once led the Xinjiang region and was previously head of China’s space program.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Stevenson
item.category: Central Commission for Discipline Inspection (Communist Party of China)
item.media:description: Ma Xingrui at a meeting in Beijing last year. He had been the Communist Party leader of the far western region of Xinjiang before his downfall.

item.title: U.S. Workers Are More Productive Than Ever. A.I. Isn’t the Key.
item.description: Companies have been getting more out of employees for several years. Tight labor markets, digitization and remote work are among the reasons.
item.dc:creator: Talmon Joseph Smith

item.title: How a Promising Navy Drone Boat Program Got Mired in Politics
item.description: Navy acquisition reforms upended a $2.1 billion contract competition. When it was over, several companies with ties to the Trump administration had made the cut.
item.dc:creator: Kate Kelly and Julie Tate
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: The Corsair, an unmanned vessel made by Saronic Technologies, demonstrated at a defense industry event last year. Saronic, which has twice made the Navy’s list of potential contract winners, has forged strong connections to allies of President Trump.

item.title: Oil Price Dips From High After Trump Changes Plans for Shipping Fees
item.description: Brent crude, the international benchmark, breached $85 per barrel for the first time in a month.
item.dc:creator: Jason Karaian, Katie Robertson and Jenny Gross
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Trump’s Strait of Hormuz Fee Could Double the Cost of Shipping
item.description: The potential expense of a 20 percent charge to move oil and other goods through the strait has stirred concern among shippers.
item.dc:creator: Peter Eavis
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Two vessels off the coast of Oman last month. President Trump did not explain exactly how the 20 percent fee would be calculated.

item.title: Trump Administration Is Snapping Up Stakes in Private Companies. Could A.I. Be Next?
item.description: Some tech executives are nervous that the administration’s recent scrutiny of artificial intelligence models could be a prelude to a demand for an ownership stake.
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson
item.media:description: Sam Altman, chief executive of OpenAI, and President Trump during a Group of 7 event last month in Évian-les-Bains, France. Mr. Altman has long floated the idea of having A.I. companies give the government equity.

item.title: The Fed’s Christopher Waller Warns of Higher Rates Soon if Inflation Stays High
item.description: Christopher J. Waller, a governor at the Federal Reserve, said he needed to see several months of lower inflation data to feel confident about the outlook.
item.dc:creator: Colby Smith
item.media:description: Christopher J. Waller, a Federal Reserve governor, said the central bank might have to raise interest rates soon if inflation stays elevated.

item.title: Paramount-Warner Bros. Merger Is Challenged in Court by California and 11 Other States
item.description: The lawsuit, filed by California and 11 other states, argues that the deal would harm movie theaters and damage America’s entertainment industry.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Mullin, David McCabe and Lauren Hirsch
item.media:credit: Mark Abramson for The New York Times
item.media:description: Paramount’s acquisition of Warner Bros. was scheduled to be completed in the third quarter this year. Above, Warner Bros. in Burbank, Calif.

item.title: U.S. Electric Vehicle Sales are Down but E.V. Chargers are Booming
item.description: Electric vehicle chargers are proliferating in Southern states as fast food restaurants, stores and other businesses try to lure customers.
item.dc:creator: Jack Ewing
item.media:credit: Gavin McIntyre for The New York Times
item.media:description: A Bojangles in Savannah, Ga., recently added E.V. charging stations outside the restaurant.

item.title: Will Oil Prices Rise as the US-Iran Truce Frays? The Answer Lies With China.
item.description: For decades, OPEC influenced the market by how much oil it produced. But China, the largest importer, is demonstrating its remarkable power over prices.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca F. Elliott and Lisa Friedman
item.category: Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A tanker at a crude oil terminal in Qingdao, China. Typically the world’s largest oil importer, China slashed purchases this spring.

item.title: Strait of Hormuz Ship Traffic Falls to Lowest Point in a Month After Strikes
item.description: Shipping activity in the critical waterway has plunged after Iran attacked a vessel, setting off U.S. strikes. President Trump said he would reinstate a blockade of Iranian ports.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Gross
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Wall Street’s Big Week for Earnings and Economic Data
item.description: Earnings season kicks off in earnest on Tuesday with big banks up first. Corporate America faces a high bar to beat investor expectations.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Ross Sorkin, Bernhard Warner, Sarah Kessler, Michael J. de la Merced, Niko Gallogly and Brian O’Keefe
item.media:description: Earnings season kicks off as corporate America faces a host of challenges.
